ocr.text: In the context of hazardous waste management, the effective treatment and disposal of medical waste is of paramount
importance for both environmental sustainability and public health. The accompanying data table delineates the volumes of
hazardous waste disposal managed by Suez SA, focusing on metrics such as On-Site Treatment and Off-Site Treatment.
Notably, the financial implications of these operations are underscored through the Total Treatment Costs, which for
CAPEX(CHF) was CHF 500,000. This figure is critical for understanding the investment required to ensure compliance with
stringent regulatory frameworks. Furthermore, the Net Income from the medical waste segment was CHF 200, which highlights
the profitability challenges faced in this sector. The operational landscape is further characterized by significant expenditures,
with Operational Costs(CHF) related to CAPEX Investments amounting to CHF 40,000. Additionally, the Revenue from Off-
Site Treatment was CHF 250,000, reflecting the reliance on diverse treatment methods to optimize waste management
strategies. The financial viability of these operations is further illustrated by the Revenue(CHF) from CAPEX Investments,
which reached CHF 80,000, and the Profit Margin(%), that recorded a substantial CHF 300,000. These metrics collectively
provide a comprehensive overview of the hazardous waste disposal landscape, illustrating the balance between treatment costs,
revenue generation, and the imperative for sustainable practices in waste management.
